queries.query: we're building a new product and have no docs yet — design the documentation information architecture, the mode taxonomy, and where the source of truth lives for each fact class
design the structure of our developer docs from scratch — README, quickstart, reference, and a cookbook — for an SDK shipping in python, node, and go
what should our REST API reference and structured error envelope look like for human integrators? design the contract docs before we ship
design the in-product help and onboarding for a new feature — empty states, tooltips, and a help-center IA — before engineering builds it
our human docs and our agent docs keep pulling in different directions — design a resolution so one source can serve both without one audience losing
define how we measure docs quality — time-to-first-hello-world, sample freshness, zero-result search rate — with thresholds, owners, and what each number triggers
set up the docs-as-code CI gates and accessibility checks for our docs program — what blocks merge, what feedback loop turns weak signals into backlog items?
define how we measure our OpenAPI schema-description quality and error-code doc coverage — retrieval evals and docs CI gates
measure help-center deflection and onboarding activation, and tie article-helpfulness signals to an owner and an action — define the metric contract
our quickstart has 9 steps and new devs keep getting stuck at auth — audit this existing README and tell me where people fall off and how bad each gap is
support keeps getting tickets that our refund example throws a 400 because the snippet is stale — diagnose why this keeps happening
our agent keeps calling the wrong MCP tool because two descriptions are nearly identical — debug which one is wrong and what to tighten
design a lower-friction consumer signup flow — the visual UI and form steps for general buyers, not docs
claude code keeps tripping on our repo — wrong test commands, stale AGENTS.md, blocked pushes. design how to make the repo agent-ready
refactor this typescript function to use early returns instead of nested if statements
we don't have a samples directory and our docs only have inline snippets — design the examples surface before launch
